name: Nevada
agency: Nevada Secretary of State, Commercial Recordings Division
address: Commercial Recordings Division, 202 North Carson Street, Carson City, NV 89701-4201

steps: Vote to dissolve per operating agreement or majority vote of members
Wind up business affairs, settle debts, and distribute remaining assets
File Certificate of Cancellation with Secretary of State ($100 fee)
File final Nevada Commerce Tax return if applicable
Separately cancel state business license through SilverFlume
Notify Nevada Department of Taxation and close tax accounts
Cancel any DBAs, licenses, and permits
llc.notes: Nevada uses Certificate of Cancellation for LLCs. If the LLC has never conducted business, a Certificate of Dissolution may be used instead. Dissolving your entity does NOT automatically cancel your state business license -- you must separately close it through SilverFlume. Expedited processing available: $125 for 24-hour, $500 for 2-hour, $1,000 for 1-hour service.

steps: Board of directors adopts resolution recommending dissolution
Shareholders approve dissolution by majority vote
Wind up business affairs, notify creditors, and settle debts
File Certificate of Dissolution with Secretary of State ($100 fee)
File final Nevada Commerce Tax return if applicable
Separately cancel state business license through SilverFlume
Notify Nevada Department of Taxation and close tax accounts
corporation.notes: Corporations that have not commenced business may use a simplified Certificate of Dissolution. Corporations that have commenced business use a different dissolution form. State business license must be separately canceled through SilverFlume.

tax.notes: Nevada has no corporate income tax or personal income tax. However, businesses may be subject to the Commerce Tax (gross revenue over $4 million) and must file a final return. State business license must be separately canceled. Close all accounts with the Department of Taxation.

requirements.employeeNotification: Follow federal WARN Act if 100+ employees
requirements.creditorNotification: Must notify known creditors in writing; unknown creditors may be notified by publication
requirements.registeredAgentNotes: Registered agent must be maintained until dissolution is complete. Nevada requires a registered agent for all business entities.
requirements.windingUpPeriod: Corporation or LLC continues for winding up purposes after dissolution filing
requirements.boardOrMemberApproval: LLCs require majority vote of members. Corporations require board resolution and majority shareholder approval.

includes: Every required form with direct download links
